title: Apollo Platform Rebuild
subtitle: A daily log of what we shipped and what's planned next while we rebuild the Apollo platform. Progress, shared materials, and every standup in one place.
footer_right: Updated daily at standup · Engagement: 12 Jan – 20 Mar 2026
milestones_note: Where each workstream stands against its target.

milestones.label: Milestone 1 — API rebuild
milestones.note: Delivered · all 24 endpoints migrated and load-tested (target was 2 Feb).

milestones.label: Milestone 2 — Data migration
milestones.note: Coverage 92.3% · Sprint 4 — day 11 of 14 · 3 tables pending schema sign-off.

milestones.label: Milestone 3 — UI modernization
milestones.note: Design system in place; 5 of 14 screens ported.

resources_note: Everything exchanged between the two teams, in one place — what we've shared and what we've received. Files download on click; external sites open in a new tab.

out.heading: Shared by Acme → Northwind
out.subheading: What we have delivered to the Northwind team

items.title: API migration overview (handout)
items.desc: How the 24 legacy endpoints map to the new gRPC surface, the auth model, and the rollback plan.

items.title: Live API reference
items.desc: Auto-generated OpenAPI docs for the rebuilt service surface.

items.title: Architecture walkthrough (12 min)
items.desc: Recorded session covering the new service boundaries and data flow.

items.title: Staging environment
items.desc: Seed accounts for the Northwind reviewers. Rotate before any real use.

credentials.note: ⚠ Seed accounts; the password is the shared demo one. Rotate before any real use.

in.heading: Shared by Northwind → Acme
in.subheading: What the Northwind team has given us

items.title: Legacy schema export
items.desc: Full DDL dump of the current production database for the migration mapping.

items.title: Acceptance-criteria board
items.desc: The living list of acceptance criteria per story.

items.title: Sprint 3 review notes
items.desc: Consolidated feedback from the last demo — 18 items, 12 resolved.
items.note: Link pending — the Northwind team will attach the shared doc after this standup.

calendar_note: Two months of work, one update per day. Click any highlighted day to see that day's entry.
timeline_note: One update per day. The most recent day is at the top; today is highlighted.

bullets: Completed the API rebuild — all 24 endpoints migrated and passing the contract tests.
Kicked off the data-migration workstream; mapped 21 of 30 tables.
days.note: Milestone 1 closed from our side. Data migration is the new focus.

bullets: Data migration reached 60% — 18 of 30 tables migrated with row-count parity checks.
Found a systemic date-timezone issue in three tables; fix identified.
days.note: Coverage climbing; timezone fix expected to land tomorrow.

days.title: Sprint 4 — day 11 of 14
bullets: Data migration at 78% (92.3% row-parity) after the timezone fix landed.
Started UI modernization — design system wired, first 5 screens ported.
Shared the API migration handout and the architecture walkthrough with Northwind.
days.note: Three tables still pending schema sign-off — flagged to the Northwind data owner.

archive.note: The project's raw journal, ingested verbatim from the working files by ingest-logs.mjs. Each file is expandable; each dated entry preserves the original content exactly.

entries.heading: ## 2026-02-05 — API rebuild complete
entries.content:
## 2026-02-05 — API rebuild complete

- Migrated all 24 endpoints to the new gRPC surface.
- Contract tests green; load test at 3x expected peak passed.
- Handed the migration overview doc to Northwind.

entries.heading: ## 2026-02-09 — Data migration 78%
entries.content:
## 2026-02-09 — Data migration 78%

- Timezone fix landed; row-parity now 92.3%.
- Started UI modernization — 5 of 14 screens ported.
- 3 tables still pending schema sign-off.

entries.heading: ### 2026-02-06 — Timezone drift
entries.content:
### 2026-02-06 — Timezone drift

Assumed all legacy timestamps were UTC; three tables stored local time.
Correction: detect tz per source table before the parity check. Never assume UTC.
